title: Sultanpur National Park
tagline: Sultanpur National Park — wildlife reserve of Haryana

overview.short: Sultanpur National Park is a national park in Haryana, India, covering approximately 1.43 km2 (0.55 sq mi).
overview.description: Sultanpur National Park is a national park in Haryana, India, covering approximately 1.43 km2 (0.55 sq mi).

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Garhi Harsaru
topPlaces.description: Garhi Harsaru is a town and railway junction in northern Gurugram district, Haryana, India. An inland container depot, Garhi Harsaru ICD, is located near the Garhi Harsaru Junction railway station.

topPlaces.name: Patli Hajipur
topPlaces.description: Patli Hajipur is a village in Gurgaon district of the Indian state of Haryana. It is located in Faruknagar taluk.

topPlaces.name: Dhankot
topPlaces.description: Dhankot is a village in Gurgaon, Gurgaon District, Haryana, in India. Dhankot is 6.842 km distance from its mandal main town Gurgaon.

topPlaces.name: Baoli Ghaus Ali Shah
topPlaces.description: Baoli Ghaus Ali Shah, also known as Ghaus Khan ki Baoli, is an 18th-century stepwell located in Farrukhnagar, Gurugram district, Haryana, India. Constructed during the reign of Mughal Emperor Farrukhsiyar, it was commissioned by Ghaus Ali Shah, a local chief who served under the emperor.

topPlaces.name: Dhorka
topPlaces.description: Dhorka is a village in Gurgaon district and the eponymous tehsil in the Indian state of Haryana. It is one of 38 villages in Gurgaon Block.

topPlaces.name: Farrukhnagar
topPlaces.description: Farrukhnagar is a small town and municipality in Farrukhnagar tehsil of Gurugram district in the Indian state of Haryana. It is situated 21 kilometres (13 mi) from Gurgaon and shares its border with Jhajjar district.

topPlaces.name: Hayatpur
topPlaces.description: Hayatpur is a village in Gurgaon Mandal, Gurgaon District, Haryana State. Hayatpur is 11.22 km distance from its Mandal Main Town Gurgaon .

topPlaces.name: Najafgarh drain bird sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Najafgarh drain bird sanctuary and wetland ecosystem is composed of the wetland ecosystem and wildlife habitat on several kilometres of the Najafgarh drain (Delhi end of Sahibi River before it falls into Yamuna) which passes through southwest Delhi in India's capital territory. It includes the portion draining the depression or basin area that formed the once famous but now partially drained Najafgarh lake (Najafgarh jheel).

items.note: Garhi Harsaru is a town and railway junction in northern Gurugram district, Haryana, India. An inland container depot, Garhi Harsaru ICD, is located near the Garhi Harsaru Junction railway station.
Patli Hajipur is a village in Gurgaon district of the Indian state of Haryana. It is located in Faruknagar taluk.
Dhankot is a village in Gurgaon, Gurgaon District, Haryana, in India. Dhankot is 6.842 km distance from its mandal main town Gurgaon.

items.note: Baoli Ghaus Ali Shah, also known as Ghaus Khan ki Baoli, is an 18th-century stepwell located in Farrukhnagar, Gurugram district, Haryana, India. Constructed during the reign of Mughal Emperor Farrukhsiyar, it was commissioned by Ghaus Ali Shah, a local chief who served under the emperor.
Dhorka is a village in Gurgaon district and the eponymous tehsil in the Indian state of Haryana. It is one of 38 villages in Gurgaon Block.

Farrukhnagar is a small town and municipality in Farrukhnagar tehsil of Gurugram district in the Indian state of Haryana. It is situated 21 kilometres (13 mi) from Gurgaon and shares its border with Jhajjar district.
Hayatpur is a village in Gurgaon Mandal, Gurgaon District, Haryana State. Hayatpur is 11.22 km distance from its Mandal Main Town Gurgaon .

Najafgarh drain bird sanctuary and wetland ecosystem is composed of the wetland ecosystem and wildlife habitat on several kilometres of the Najafgarh drain (Delhi end of Sahibi River before it falls into Yamuna) which passes through southwest Delhi in India's capital territory. It includes the portion draining the depression or basin area that formed the once famous but now partially drained Najafgarh lake (Najafgarh jheel).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sultanpur National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sultanpur National Park is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sultanpur National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Indira Gandhi International Airport, Delhi (23 km) and the nearest railway station is New Delhi Railway Station (38 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sultanpur National Park?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sultanpur National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sultanpur National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Sultanpur National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sultanpur National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Garhi Harsaru, Patli Hajipur, Dhankot, Baoli Ghaus Ali Shah, Dhorka.
